mappings.rationaleAI: The Prester John legend was directly identified with Ethiopia by Portuguese explorers, connecting to the Solomonid dynasty (B.2) and motivating Portuguese contacts with Ethiopia from 1520 onward (B.3).

The seventh-century Arab conquest of Egypt and North Africa initiated Muslim pressure on Christian Nubia, directly relevant to the spread of Muslim domination from c. 639 (A.2), and reshaped the Aksumite empire's relations with neighbouring Islamic states (B.1).

Coffee's Ethiopian origins and its spread through the Ottoman Empire connect to Ethiopian cultural and economic history within the section's timeframe, providing context for Ethiopia's place in broader trade networks.
